summary: India's National Population Register (NPR) is a comprehensive database of usual residents, collected under the Citizenship Act, 1955 and maintained by the Office of the Registrar General and Census Commissioner. The NPR was last updated during Census 2011 and a subsequent update exercise in 2015. While the dataset exists and is linked to Aadhaar numbers, there is no citizen-facing digital portal for individuals to view or update their population register records. The data primarily serves administrative and planning purposes rather than functioning as a real-time digital population register.

sources.title: Office of the Registrar General & Census Commissioner - NPR

notes: The NPR update and Census 2021 exercises were delayed due to COVID-19 and have faced further postponements. While Aadhaar provides a de facto digital identity register, it is technically an identity verification system rather than a population register. The lack of an updated, real-time, citizen-facing digital population register distinguishes India from countries like Estonia or Finland.
